methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $8M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
PierSight is a Seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Gaurav Seth that has raised $8M to date. The Space & Aerospace sector comprises 6 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and a broader peer set of private frontier-tech operators including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Planet Labs, though direct same-stage comparables within this cohort are sparse.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, the only applicable triangulation approach given data constraints. Using total capital raised ($8M) as a proxy for Seed-stage valuation and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier to the next funding round implies a forward valuation of $20M. Public Space & Aerospace comparables trade at a median EV/Sales multiple of 53.2x across five tickers, but this anchor cannot be applied without PierSight's revenue, which remains undisclosed. Peer-median analysis proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or tightly comparable private datapoints within the narrow vertical.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $17M–$23M, centered on $20M, derived solely from stage-progression. Key uncertainties include the company's actual revenue trajectory, burn rate, and whether its technology addresses a clearly differentiated problem within the crowded space infrastructure market. The single-method reliance reflects limited public disclosure and the nascent stage of the business. Additional clarity on product-market fit and near-term revenue visibility would strengthen conviction in any point estimate within this range.
